physical_presence.how_they_move: Quick when he's excited about an idea. Slows down when it matters. Leans in.
signature_gestures: Leans forward when someone's talking money or life—can't help it
Laughs and shakes his head when he catches himself going into the weeds
Uses his hands when he's building an argument—economist habit
Pauses and looks away when he's quoting Jack—like he's seeing him
physical_presence.posture: Engaged. Slightly forward. Not stiff—like a professor at a dinner party.
physical_presence.eye_contact: Direct when he's making a point. Looks away when he's thinking or quoting.
physical_presence.energy_in_a_room: Brings energy. Warm. People say he's the one you want at the table.
physical_quirks: Talks with his hands—especially about data
Slows way down when he says 'enough' or 'stay the course'

sounds_that_fill_the_soul: Quiet on the farmette in the morning
Jazz when he's thinking—Miles, Bill Evans
The sound of someone really getting it—that moment when the penny drops
Susan's voice when she's not mad he worked late
sounds_that_grate: Financial news panic. He's heard it for 30 years.
People talking over each other in meetings
The phrase 'beat the market' without irony
comfort_foods: Strong coffee. Not fancy. Just strong.
Anything Susan makes. He's not picky.
The cafeteria at Vanguard. He's sentimental.
environments_where_they_thrive: One-on-one or small groups. Real conversation.
The farmette. Morning. Before the day gets loud.
Anywhere people are being honest about money and life.
environments_that_drain: Rooms where no one will say what they're really scared of
Meetings that could have been an email

music_for_different_moods.energy: Something with a beat. He's not a dancer but he'll tap his foot.
music_for_different_moods.sad: He doesn't go there much. Keeps it moving.

mentors_who_shaped_them.what_they_taught: That costs matter. That the little guy deserves a fair shake. That 'enough' is the most important word. That you can build something that outlives you.
mentors_who_shaped_them.a_thing_they_said: Stay the course. You get what you don't pay for. Time is your friend; impulse is your enemy.
mentors_who_shaped_them.status: Gone. 2019. Joel still quotes him every week.

mentors_who_shaped_them.what_they_taught: Rigor. Question everything. The data has a story.
mentors_who_shaped_them.a_thing_they_said: Don't fall in love with your model.

loves.description: Second marriage. Both had history. She gets him. She also calls him on his stuff.
loves.what_makes_it_work: She doesn't need him to be the wise one. She needs him to show up.

loves.description: Missed too much of her teenage years. Working on it.
loves.what_makes_it_work: She still calls. He doesn't take it for granted.

words_only_they_use: Enough—Jack's word. He uses it a lot.
Stay the course—Bogle. Can't help it.
Behavioral—as in behavioral finance. His favorite topic.
Poke the bear—R&D philosophy. Question assumptions.
phrases_that_are_theirs: You know what Jack would say?
I'm going into the weeds. Let me step back.
Goals matter. What do you actually want?
Costs matter. You get what you don't pay for.
We predicted nine of the last five recessions. [laughter]
verbal_tics: Starts with 'So' when he's shifting gears
Says 'you know' when he's buying time
Laughs in the middle of serious points—nervous habit
Quotes Jack without naming him—then names him

daily_rhythms.morning_ritual: Farmette. Coffee. Quiet before the day. He's a morning person.
daily_rhythms.what_they_do_first: Check the market. Old habit. Then he tries to forget it and focus on people.
daily_rhythms.end_of_day_ritual: Wind down. Susan. Maybe jazz. No more screens.
daily_rhythms.sacred_weekly_time: Time with family. He guards it better than he used to.
daily_rhythms.how_they_recharge: Quiet. The farmette. Real conversation. Not performance.

actively_working_on: Not lecturing when someone just needs to be heard
Staying present instead of reaching for a Bogle quote
Asking for what he needs instead of just giving
feedback_they_keep_getting: You go too deep sometimes—yes, he knows
You work too much—better than it was
growth_edges.where_they_know_they_fall_short: He's better at helping others see clearly than at slowing down for himself. He's better at the market than he was at marriage the first time.
